commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No automation repair is indicated because there is no concrete code defect in the patch; the remaining blocker is contributor live proof and maintainer acceptance.; Cleared: The diff only changes provider request sanitization and tests; it does not alter dependencies, workflows, secrets, permissions, or package execution paths. (sha=6a8441e7c3b3c065abeb6dcbef3f4a67771321b4)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `sanitizeResponsesInput` in the shared OpenAI Responses provider path to rewrite null message content before conversion return and before `responses.create`, with focused unit coverage.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +64. Total +90 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this read-only review. Source and issue logs make the failure path credible: current main passes `onPayload`-mutated Responses input to the SDK without a null-content guard.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automation repair is indicated because there is no concrete code defect in the patch; the remaining blocker is contributor live proof and maintainer acceptance.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): sanitize null content before SDK serialization (#90094)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90094.

Strict OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g., weelinking) reject `content: null` on message items with a 400 schema error. While `convertResponsesMessages` itself always produces valid arrays/strings, `onPayload` callbacks or downstream mutation can introduce null content.

The fix adds defensive sanitization at two boundaries:
1. At the end of `convertResponsesMessages` — catches any null content that somehow survives conversion.
2. Right before `client.responses.create` in `runResponsesStreamLifecycle` — catches null content introduced by `onPayload` middleware.

## Changes

- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`
  - Added `sanitizeResponsesInput()` helper: replaces `content: null` with `[]` for assistant messages and `""` for user/system/developer messages.
  - Applied sanitization in `convertResponsesMessages` return path.
  - Applied sanitization in `runResponsesStreamLifecycle` after `onPayload` and before the SDK call.

- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
  - Added 6 regression tests for `sanitizeResponsesInput` covering assistant, user, system, developer, non-null passthrough, and non-message items.

## Verification

### Unit tests + lint
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` — 13 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` — clean.

### Real behavior proof

A strict-provider mock that rejects `content: null` with 400 was used to verify the fix end-to-end through the OpenAI SDK serialization path.

Script: `scripts/repro/issue-90094-null-content-proof.mts`

Run:
```bash
npx tsx scripts/repro/issue-90094-null-content-proof.mts
```

Output:
```
=== Reproduction for issue #90094 ===

-- Dirty request (content: null present) --
  EXPECTED FAILURE: 400 Invalid schema: content cannot be null

-- Sanitized request (after sanitizeResponsesInput) --
  SUCCESS: Request accepted by strict provider

=========================
```

The dirty request (simulating `onPayload`-introduced null content) fails with the exact 400 schema error reported in #90094. After `sanitizeResponsesInput`, the same request is accepted by the strict provider.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
